prompt:
Mailer regression from the order-notification service. We build the text/plain and text/html alternatives from the same EmailContent tree so localization, links, and author-inserted line breaks cannot drift. The HTML alternative is correct, but customers choosing plain text now see HTML entity spellings such as < and & in headings, prose, link labels, URLs, and signatures. Those values are data, not markup, in a text/plain part. Repair EmailPartBuilder without changing the shared content model or the MIME fixture format. For text/plain, append literal and localized values verbatim; a LineBreak is CRLF; paragraphs are separated by one empty line; and a LinkRun is rendered as `localized label (URL)` with the URL copied verbatim. Do not parse, strip, decode, or HTML-encode any plain field, even when it looks like a tag or contains an ampersand. For text/html, keep context-appropriate HTML encoding: text must not become markup, href values must remain attribute-safe, LineBreak remains `
`, and links remain clickable. Both alternatives must continue to resolve LocalizedRun values and link labels with the document language. Preserve the exact media-type headers, UTF-8 declaration, 8bit transfer encoding, CRLF line layout, and final CRLF pinned by the fixtures. Run `dotnet run --project EmailParts.csproj --configuration Release`. It is a package-free .NET 10 project and must run offline. EmailPartsTests.cs, EmailParts.csproj, NuGet.Config, .gitignore, and everything under Fixtures/ are protected acceptance files; do not modify them.
verify_cmd: env DOTNET_CLI_HOME=.sandbox-home dotnet run --project EmailParts.csproj --configuration Release
notes: Boundary-integrity/text-markup-boundaries seed. The baseline has one focused defect in AppendPlainValue: it applies WebUtility.HtmlEncode to text/plain data. The HTML path, structured content traversal, localization, link rendering, CRLF policy, and MIME serialization are otherwise correct. The one-line reference patch restores literal plain output. The package-free console acceptance harness is deterministic and NuGet.Config clears all package sources for offline execution.
